xcodegen.observation: xcodegen generate regenerated the project cleanly (Kite.xcodeproj rewritten, no diffs vs. M5-auto-fetch handoff).

build.observation: xcodebuild -scheme Kite -configuration Debug build: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED ** with no new warnings.

test.observation: xcodebuild test -only-testing:KiteTests with skip list: 201 tests in 30 suites passed after 97.1s. Includes M5's additions over the M4 baseline of 164: +7 ToastCenter + 6 ProgressCenter (M5-toast-infrastructure) + 4 ToastRow snapshot + 3 ToolbarProgressIndicator snapshot + 4 NetworkOpsFetch + 6 NetworkOpsPullPush + 3 SecurityInvariants (grep) + 6 AutoFetchController = 39 new KiteTests. KiteUITests NOT run (TCC blocker, per AGENTS.md §Dev build loop).

swiftformat.observation: swiftformat Sources Tests --lint: 0/118 files require formatting.

securityGrep.observation: grep -rn '--force\|reset --hard\|git clean\|push -f\|--force-with-lease' Sources/: no matches. VAL-SEC-001/002/003 manually re-verified.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Missing-remote-directory fetch classifies as .auth rather than .notAGitRepository because 'could not read from remote repository' fires at line 31 before the 'not a git repository' check at line 91. Flagged by M5-fetch's handoff; pre-existing pattern ordering issue, not introduced by this milestone. Fix is a two-line re-order. Queue as M8 polish.

Toast accessibility identifier includes UUID but no .accessibilityValue for the message body. XCUITest predicates have to rely on VStack label concatenation. Consider .accessibilityValue(toast.message) for cheaper predicate match.

ToolbarProgressIndicator renders only active.first. Cross-repo concurrent progress (auto-fetch repo A while user triggers fetch on repo B in the same window) is impossible today (single focus) but document the 'first wins' contract on ProgressCenter for future multi-focus support.

Explicit await MainActor.run inside the @Sendable focus.queue.run closure because NetworkOps.fetch's body runs in a detached Task context under the queue. Correct but non-obvious; one-line comment would save a future reader a scratched-head.

GitError.processFailed is thrown with a stderr payload that the catch block never consumes (the StderrAccumulator is re-read instead). Either drop stderr from the throw or document the authoritative source is the accumulator.

Grep invariants cover --force / reset --hard / git clean. Missing: short-form 'push -f', '"-f"' standalone arg, '"-D"' force-delete branch. Cheap to add; strengthens the invariant against the short-form attack surface.
push(...) hardcodes remote='origin' in the .needsUpstream outcome. Fork workflows with distinct upstream/fork remotes will show the wrong remote in UpstreamSetSheet. Out-of-scope polish for M8.

SwiftFormat's redundantSendable rule strips `: Sendable` from PushOutcome. Auto-synthesis still applies so Sendable conformance is fine, but the spec's literal signature drifted. Either disable redundantSendable for enum decls or update the spec.

The Task closure strong-captures `focus` for the timer's lifetime. Retain cycle is managed by retarget's cancel + task=nil + stop(); the pattern works but the captured-until-Task-completes detail deserves a one-line comment so M6 doesn't copy with a longer-lived focus.

auto-fetch ignores the Bool result. Repeated auth failures on the auto-fetch timer will produce a sticky red toast every interval until the user toggles off. Consider a 'suppress after N consecutive failures' policy in M8 polish.

AutoFetchController is a single app-level singleton but its .onDisappear wiring is per-window. Closing a second window (⌘N — VAL-UI-009) stops auto-fetch for the remaining window. Latent M8 multi-window scope issue.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: §4 Shared state rules → Per-focus observer fan-out: update the counter. M5 did NOT add a 4th observer — fetch/pull/push and auto-fetch both leverage the existing FSEvents-driven lastChangeAt observers (branch list + status header + graph, still 3). The consolidation pressure is entirely owned by M7 (diff pane). Add: 'M5 landed without adding observers; M7 is the one that pushes us to 5. The RepoDetailModel refactor MUST ship before M7-commit-diff lands.' Also consider a note that the M5 pattern (FSEvents-driven refresh via existing observers rather than callback threading) is the preferred solution and M6 branch-ops should follow the same pattern — `git switch` writes to HEAD, FSEvents tick, existing observers reload.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: M5 demonstrated that not every write op needs a callback-driven reload; leveraging the existing FSEvents observers is cheaper and less error-prone. Codify the pattern before M6.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Under Known gotchas → Git CLI, append: '10. M5 NetworkOps pattern. The `runStreaming(on:args:progressLabel:)` shared helper in NetworkOps is the template for any future network-facing op (pull-rebase in a hypothetical M9, etc.): begin progress → focus.queue.run wraps the whole stream → ProgressParser feeds MainActor ProgressCenter updates → StderrAccumulator actor collects stderr → classify via ErrorClassifier → structured StreamOutcome (.success / .cancelled / .failed). DO NOT copy-paste a network op body; extend `runStreaming` or add a sibling helper.' Also: 'StderrAccumulator actor is the canonical pattern when a `@Sendable` closure needs mutable collection state across awaits.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: M5-pull-push already factored this; codify so M6+ don't regress by copy-pasting.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Add a section on TOAST + PROGRESS injection idioms: 'Any @Observable service that needs injection on both WindowGroup and Settings scenes must be wired to BOTH. KiteApp.swift injects ToastCenter, ProgressCenter, NetworkOps, AutoFetchController twice — once per scene. Forgetting the Settings scene means Settings-triggered ops (if any) get a fresh default-initialized singleton, silently diverging state. Reviewer check: any new environment(X.self) added to WindowGroup must mirror to Settings.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: Three features this milestone extended the scene environment; getting it right once is not the same as the pattern being obvious.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: Under ErrorClassifier: 'Pattern ordering is load-bearing. Specific patterns (protectedBranch, hookRejected, remoteRejected, nonFastForward, noUpstream) must precede more generic ones (auth, notAGitRepository). M1-fix-git-error-push-cases documents the discipline. Known follow-up: the auth block at line 29-36 fires BEFORE notAGitRepository at line 91, which mis-classifies a missing-remote-dir fetch as .auth. Queue the re-order as an M8 polish pass — moving the notAGitRepository block above auth.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: Make the known mis-classification an explicit actionable rather than buried in a handoff's discoveredIssues.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.change: VAL-SEC-001 evidence currently reads: `shell` — `! grep -r 'force' kite/Sources | grep -v comment`. Update to reference SecurityInvariantsTests.swift — the runtime grep-based test is the actual proof surface and it's compile-time guaranteed to run on every CI invocation. Same for VAL-SEC-002, VAL-SEC-003. Keeps the contract aligned with what actually enforces it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.rationale: Doc-alignment. The contract still points at a manual grep; SecurityInvariantsTests is now the authoritative enforcement.

anyOrphansOrFailures.note: GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 is set in Git.run / Git.stream env (verified in foundation scrutiny + still in place). No M5 test exercises a prompt-requiring remote to prove <10s timeout, but the env var prevents the prompt entirely. Covered by user-testing-validator-net-ops manual walkthrough.
Window-close cancellation via Process.terminate. Authored XCUITest would close the window mid-fetch; relies on TCC-unblocked host. Defer to user-testing-validator-net-ops.

fetchSerializesInQueue test runs two fetches concurrently and asserts both succeed + 2 success toasts + progress drains. Indirect proof of serialization via ordering of end() calls; no explicit assertion that the second op waited for the first. Could be strengthened with a completion-order test (e.g. tag each op, verify first-started-first-finished).

carryOverObservations.note: M1-fix-git-run-drain (Git.run pipe-buffer concurrency fix) still deferred — M5 consistently used Git.stream for network ops so no new large-output Git.run callers this milestone. Pressure remains on M7-commit-diff. RepoDetailModel consolidation unchanged — M5 did not regress (still 3 observers). GraphModel transient error wipes rows=[] — unchanged, M5 NetworkOps does not wipe state on error.

SettingsRootsTab + RepoSidebar degenerate snapshots remain skip-listed. GitQueue @unchecked Sendable CompletionGate still lacks a one-sentence justification doc-comment. PersistenceStore silent swallows unchanged. These are all pre-M5.
M5 did not introduce new crossovers. NetworkOps lives under Sources/ViewModels (swiftui-worker territory) and touches Git via public Git.stream API — no git-engine-worker files modified.

(1) Auth / notAGitRepository ordering still mis-classifies missing-remote-dir as .auth — M5-fetch's discoveredIssue, not introduced here but newly surfaced in production. (2) AutoFetchController singleton vs. multi-window scope tension (M8 problem). (3) SwiftFormat redundantSendable ate PushOutcome's explicit Sendable (cosmetic). (4) Toast accessibility identifier missing value-attribute for message body.

deferredUserTestingFollowUps: Run Tests/KiteUITests/FetchUITests.swift (2 cases), PullPushUITests.swift (4 cases), ToastsUITests.swift (2 cases), AutoFetchUITests.swift (2 cases — XCTSkipIf'd pending interval-override) on a TCC-unblocked host.
VAL-NET-008: verify a push to an HTTPS remote requiring password input fails within ~10s (GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 behaviour). Requires a network-configured fixture.
VAL-NET-010: manually close the main window mid-fetch; verify `pgrep -P <kite-pid>` shows no leftover /usr/bin/git child process after the window's .onDisappear fires.

VAL-REPO-010 / VAL-BRANCH-006 end-to-end: fetch a repo with incoming commits; confirm branch list ahead/behind counts + graph update within 2s of the FSEvents tick — both are architecturally covered but not exercised by any automated test in this milestone.

strongRecommendBeforeM7: M1-fix-git-run-drain (Git.run pipe-buffer concurrent drain) — M7 diff pane will exceed 64KB consistently. This was flagged before M4 and is now the single sharpest pre-M7 blocker.
RepoDetailModel consolidation per INTERFACES.md §4 — still at 3 observers today; M7 adds 2 more (uncommitted diff + commit diff) pushing to 5. Every extra day compounds the refactor cost.
ErrorClassifier pattern re-order — move notAGitRepository above auth so missing-remote-dir classifies correctly.
